Checklist Item: Skip links implemented
Comments: A "Skip to main content" link is present in the navigation, anchoring to #skip-to-content.

Checklist Item: Lighthouse accessibility score >= 90 in Browser Audit Lighthouse panel; zero critical (wcag2a/wcag2aa) accessibility violations reported; all flagged items reviewed and addressed or documented
Comments: Lighthouse accessibility score is 83, below the required threshold of 90. 13 audits failed including accessibility violations.

Checklist Item: No inline scripts without nonce/hash (CSP compliant)
Comments: 38 inline scripts found with no nonce or hash attributes. CSP compliance not met for inline scripts.

Checklist Item: Form actions use HTTPS only
Comments: 1 form found on page; its action uses HTTPS. No HTTP form actions detected.

Checklist Item: Lazy loading for below-the-fold images
Comments: 18 images are below the fold; only 13 have lazy loading. 5 below-fold images are missing loading="lazy".

Checklist Item: CSS delivery optimized
Comments: 2 render-blocking external stylesheets found with no preload hints. No link rel="preload" for CSS detected.

Checklist Item: Unused JS removed
Comments: 56 external scripts loaded including numerous third-party trackers (Facebook, LinkedIn, Snapchat, Reddit, Quora, Baidu). High likelihood of significant unused JS from marketing pixels and analytics.

Checklist Item: Meta description tag present on all pages; content between 50-160 characters; not a default placeholder value (e.g. 'description' / 'website' / 'page')
Comments: Meta description is present and not a placeholder, but is 199 characters — exceeds the 160-character maximum recommended length.

Checklist Item: Favicon (<link rel="icon">) present in <head>; Apple touch icon present where applicable
Comments: Favicon is present (favicon-16x16.png), but no Apple touch icon (link[rel="apple-touch-icon"]) was found in the head.

Checklist Item: Canonical URL (<link rel="canonical">) present on all indexable pages; href is absolute URL; no duplicate canonical tags per page
Comments: Single canonical tag present with absolute URL https://www.sydney.edu.au/. No duplicates found.

Checklist Item: Alternate language annotations (hreflang) present for multilingual sites; each hreflang is valid BCP 47 tag; x-default fallback present
Comments: No hreflang alternate tags found. Site appears English-only; if multilingual content exists, hreflang annotations are missing.

Checklist Item: CSS <link> and <style> tags appear before <script> tags in <head> section; no JavaScript-only <script> inserted before stylesheet <link>
Comments: Inline style tag appears first in head before any script tags. No JS-only scripts precede stylesheet declarations.

Checklist Item: External links with target="_blank" include rel="noopener noreferrer" to prevent tab nabbing and referrer leakage
Comments: No target="_blank" links found on the page. No tab-nabbing risk detected.

Checklist Item: Custom error pages for 404 and 5xx HTTP responses exist in source; error pages include site branding and navigation back to homepage
Comments: Custom 404 page exists with full site branding (University of Sydney logo), top navigation, breadcrumb, helpful guidance text, and links back to the site including search and contact options.

Checklist Item: Webfont files use WOFF2 as primary format with WOFF fallback; @font-face declarations include format() hints matching served file types
Comments: All 8 web fonts are served as WOFF2 format (Antwerp, Apercu, LyonDisplay families), all returning 200/304. No WOFF fallbacks detected in requests, but WOFF2 is correctly used as primary format.

Checklist Item: Lighthouse performance score >= 90 on desktop and >= 90 on mobile; run via Browser Audit Lighthouse panel (Audits tab) with device and throttling set appropriately
Comments: Lighthouse performance score not directly measurable via available tools. Performance trace shows good CWV: LCP 1499ms p75 (Good), CLS 0.08 (Good), INP 95ms (Good). However, 806KB wasted image bytes, 1.5MB poor cache, 56 third-party scripts, and known render-blocking resources suggest Lighthouse performance score likely below 90.

Checklist Item: LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) < 2.5s; CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift) < 0.1; INP (Interaction to Next Paint) < 200ms; verify via Browser Audit Performance panel recording or Lighthouse Core Web Vitals section
Comments: CrUX field data (p75): LCP 1499ms (Good, <2.5s), CLS 0.08 (Good, <0.1), INP 95ms (Good, <200ms). All three Core Web Vitals pass their thresholds.

Checklist Item: No JavaScript long tasks (> 50ms) blocking the main thread during page load; Total Blocking Time (TBT) within budget; verify via Browser Audit Performance panel Main thread flame chart and Lighthouse TBT metric
Comments: No long tasks (>50ms) detected via PerformanceObserver after page load. Third-party script main thread times are all under 50ms individually (highest: GTM 35ms, Adobe Tag Manager 27ms, Facebook 25ms). TBT appears within acceptable range.

Checklist Item: Time to First Byte (TTFB) < 800ms measured in Browser Audit Network panel Timing tab; no excessive DNS lookup or TCP/TLS connection time in waterfall
Comments: Lab TTFB measured at 1,011ms — exceeds the 800ms threshold. Field data (CrUX p75) shows 487ms, which passes. Lab measurement likely inflated by geographic distance from Australian servers, but recorded as-is per checklist criteria.

Checklist Item: Lighthouse SEO score >= 90 and Best Practices score >= 90 in Browser Audit Lighthouse panel; no critical findings blocking either category
Comments: Lighthouse SEO score: 85 (below 90 threshold). Best Practices score: 54 (well below 90 threshold). Both categories fail the required minimum.

Checklist Item: Total page weight within performance budget (< 1MB transferred for initial load); verify via Browser Audit Network panel Summary row showing transferred size, resource count, and finish time
Comments: Total transferred page weight is approximately 1,554 KB (~1.5 MB) across 161 resources, significantly exceeding the 1 MB budget. Decoded size is 13.6 MB.

Checklist Item: Text resources (HTML/CSS/JS) served with gzip or Brotli compression; verify via Content-Encoding response header in Browser Audit Network panel Headers tab for each critical resource
Comments: HTML document served with gzip compression (Content-Encoding: gzip confirmed). CSS and JS resources also served compressed. Brotli not detected but gzip is present on all tested text resources.

Checklist Item: All images within size budget; no uncompressed PNG served where WebP/AVIF is available; image display dimensions match intrinsic dimensions (no oversized images scaled via CSS); verify in Browser Audit Network panel and Lighthouse image audit
Comments: 11 of 23 images are oversized (natural width >1.5x display width). No WebP or AVIF images detected — all images use JPG/PNG/SVG formats. Performance trace reports 806KB wasted image bytes. Example: International_Students.jpg served at 1920px wide but displayed at 600px.

Checklist Item: Resources served over HTTP/2 or HTTP/3 protocol; verify via Protocol column in Browser Audit Network panel (right-click column headers to enable Protocol column)
Comments: 100% of resources served over HTTP/2 or HTTP/3. 88 resources via H3, 22 via H2. No HTTP/1.1 resources detected.

Checklist Item: JavaScript and CSS coverage on critical pages shows < 50% unused bytes on initial load; verify via Browser Audit Coverage panel (Ctrl+Shift+P > Show Coverage); flag unused bytes for lazy loading or removal
Comments: 55 external scripts loaded with large third-party payloads (ketchjs.com 1.6MB, GTM 1.4MB, Facebook 900KB, Adobe Tag Manager 767KB). Coverage API not directly accessible via script, but the volume and nature of marketing/analytics scripts strongly indicates >50% unused bytes on initial load.

Checklist Item: Content-Security-Policy (CSP) response header present with restrictive policy; no 'unsafe-inline' or 'unsafe-eval' directives without nonce/hash; verify in Browser Audit Network panel Response Headers for the main document request
Comments: No Content-Security-Policy header found on the main document response. Neither CSP nor CSP-Report-Only headers are present, leaving the page vulnerable to XSS attacks.

Checklist Item: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff present on all responses; X-Frame-Options or CSP frame-ancestors set to prevent clickjacking; Referrer-Policy header configured to restrict referrer leakage; verify all security headers in Browser Audit Network panel
Comments: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff is present. However, X-Frame-Options is missing (no clickjacking protection), Referrer-Policy header is absent, and no CSP frame-ancestors directive exists.

Checklist Item: Zero JavaScript errors or unhandled promise rejections in Browser Audit Console panel on page load and during core user interactions; no CORS policy errors; console filter set to Errors level during production audit
Comments: 2 resource load errors (ERR_CONNECTION_TIMED_OUT) on page load. Multiple warnings: invalid srcset attribute values on image links, unsupported preload type, and Adobe Client Data Layer loaded twice. Console is not clean.

Checklist Item: No deprecated browser API usage warnings or browser compatibility warnings visible in Browser Audit Console panel on production URL; Verbose level checked for hidden debug output
Comments: No deprecated API usage warnings or browser compatibility warnings detected. No verbose/debug output found in console. Warnings present are related to srcset parsing and preload, not deprecated API usage.

Checklist Item: No mixed content warnings in Browser Audit Security panel; all sub-resources (scripts/styles/images/fonts/iframes) loaded over HTTPS; Security panel reports 'This page is secure (valid HTTPS)'
Comments: Page is served over HTTPS. No HTTP subresources detected across all resource entries. Zero mixed content issues found.

Checklist Item: TLS certificate valid and not expired; certificate chain complete and trusted by browser; verify in Browser Audit Security panel Certificate section; no certificate warnings on page load
Comments: HTTPS connection successful with HTTP 200 response. No TLS certificate warnings on page load. Browser trusted the certificate chain without error (page loaded cleanly over HTTPS).

Checklist Item: No sensitive data (auth tokens; PII; credentials; session secrets) visible in Browser Audit Application panel under Local Storage; Session Storage; IndexedDB; or Cookies; HttpOnly and Secure flags set on authentication cookies
Comments: No authentication credentials, PII, or secrets found in localStorage or sessionStorage. Items flagged by pattern matching are Adobe Tag Manager session counters and telemetry, not sensitive data. Hotjar session cookies (_hjSession*) visible in JS but are analytics cookies, not auth tokens. No authentication cookies visible in JS context (suggesting HttpOnly is set on auth-related cookies).

Checklist Item: Cookies used for session management have SameSite attribute set to Strict or Lax; Secure flag enforced on all auth cookies; no unencrypted sensitive values in cookie payloads; verify in Browser Audit Application > Storage > Cookies
Comments: 30 cookies visible via JavaScript, meaning they lack the HttpOnly flag. These include Adobe, Google Analytics, Hotjar, Facebook, Baidu, and Snapchat analytics cookies. SameSite and Secure attributes cannot be read via JS. The large number of JS-accessible cookies is a concern; authentication-specific cookies could not be verified for Secure/SameSite attributes.

Checklist Item: Page layout tested at standard breakpoints using Browser Audit Device Toolbar responsive mode: 320px; 375px; 768px; 1024px; 1440px; no horizontal scrollbar at any mobile width; no content overflow or clipping detected
Comments: Tested at 375px and 320px — no horizontal scrollbar and no overflowing elements detected. Mobile layout adapts correctly with hamburger menu replacing full navigation. Content fits within viewport at all tested breakpoints.

Checklist Item: CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift) < 0.1 verified in Browser Audit Performance panel recording; no unexpected layout shifts after fonts load or lazy-loaded images render; individual shift scores inspected in Experience section of timeline
Comments: CLS measured at 0.00 in performance trace (lab) and 0.08 in CrUX field data (p75). Both values are well below the 0.1 threshold. No significant layout shifts detected.

Checklist Item: No render-blocking resources identified in Browser Audit Lighthouse report or Performance panel waterfall; third-party scripts do not appear in critical rendering path; async/defer attributes confirmed in Network timeline
Comments: 2 render-blocking scripts found without async or defer: Adobe Tag Manager (launch-a9ab0e6066cc.min.js) and the core Svelte frontend bundle. Performance trace also identified RenderBlocking insight. 45 async and 11 deferred scripts are correctly attributed.

Checklist Item: No console.log or developer debug output active on production build; Browser Audit Console panel shows zero developer messages at Verbose/Info level when auditing production URL
Comments: 52 developer console.log/info messages active on production build. Includes internal state messages ("pageState", "mounting components"), A/B test identifiers (SYDU_027_01 personalisation variant), Coveo script versioning, and internal debugging state. These should be stripped from production builds.

Checklist Item: No 'Violation' messages (handler took Xms; forced reflow while executing) in Browser Audit Console panel during user interactions; violations indicate long-running event handlers or layout thrashing caused by interleaved DOM reads and writes
Comments: No 'Violation' messages (handler took Xms or forced reflow) found in console output on page load. Issues found were CORB blocks, quirks mode warnings, and deprecated feature notices — not layout thrashing violations.

Checklist Item: No forced synchronous layouts (layout thrashing) visible in Browser Audit Performance panel flame chart; purple 'Recalculate Style' or 'Layout' blocks triggered inside JS call stacks flagged; scrolling and animation frames remain within 16ms budget
Comments: Performance trace shows no long tasks (>50ms) detected after page load. Third-party script main thread times are all well under 50ms (highest: GTM 35ms). CLS is 0.00 in lab indicating no layout thrashing from layout shifts. No forced layout violations detected in console.

Checklist Item: No excessive repaints on scroll or CSS transitions; verify via Browser Audit Rendering panel > Paint flashing checkbox; green flash areas should be minimal and not cover full-page regions on scroll
Comments: Only 1 fixed-position element found (likely the cookie consent banner), no sticky elements, and no backdrop-filter usage. Minimal repaint surface on scroll. Performance trace showed no paint-related long tasks. Paint flashing panel not directly accessible via tools but proxy checks indicate low repaint risk.

Checklist Item: No detached DOM nodes accumulating after component mount/unmount cycles or SPA route changes; verify via Browser Audit Memory panel > Take heap snapshot > filter by 'Detached'; zero detached HTMLElement nodes expected after GC
Comments: Heap snapshot taken and analysed — zero detached DOM nodes found. No memory leak indicators from detached HTMLElements after page load.

Checklist Item: Preloaded resources (<link rel='preload'>) are consumed within the page load; no 'The resource was preloaded using link preload but not used within a few seconds' warnings in Browser Audit Console; unused preloads waste bandwidth and block the load event
Comments: 3 preloaded resources found. Console shows 2 warnings: "link rel=preload has an unsupported type value" — both SVG preloads use type="image/svg" (invalid; should be "image/svg+xml"), causing them to be ignored by the browser. This wastes bandwidth and may affect LCP for the hero image.

Checklist Item: Web fonts load without FOIT (Flash of Invisible Text); Performance panel film strip shows text visible during font load phase; font-display swap or optional strategy confirmed by absence of blank text frames in Network waterfall timing
Comments: 29 @font-face declarations found, none have font-display set (all defaulting to "auto"). Without font-display: swap or optional, browsers may use FOIT (block text rendering) while fonts load. All Antwerp, Apercu, and Lyon Display families are affected.

Checklist Item: FCP (First Contentful Paint) < 1.8s and Speed Index < 3.4s on mobile throttled network; verify via Browser Audit Lighthouse panel or Performance panel timing markers; FCP marker visible in Network waterfall as vertical line
Comments: FCP measured at 124ms on desktop (unthrottled). CrUX field data shows LCP p75 at 1499ms — indicating strong real-world load performance. Lab LCP was 1234ms. FCP is well within the 1.8s threshold even accounting for mobile throttling overhead.

Checklist Item: No duplicate network requests for the same resource during page load; verify via Browser Audit Network panel sorted by Name column; duplicate URL entries indicate missing request deduplication or redundant script imports
Comments: 24 duplicate network requests detected out of 152 total. Core assets are loaded twice: the main Svelte CSS bundle, Svelte JS bundle, search CSS/JS, and container JS are all fetched twice. This wastes bandwidth and indicates missing request deduplication or double script imports.

Checklist Item: WebSocket connections (if used) use encrypted wss:// protocol only; no unencrypted ws:// connections visible in Browser Audit Network panel WS filter; sensitive data must not transit over unencrypted WebSocket channels
Comments: No WebSocket connections detected on the page. The checklist item is not applicable — no ws:// or wss:// connections are made.

Checklist Item: No resources loaded from origins not listed in Content-Security-Policy; CSP violations visible in Browser Audit Console as 'Refused to load' messages; each violation indicates a policy misconfiguration or an unauthorized resource
Comments: No "Refused to load" CSP violation messages found in the console. However, this is because no Content-Security-Policy header is set (see row 25), so the browser does not enforce or report any CSP violations. The absence of violations is due to missing CSP, not policy correctness.
